title: phaseolus panduratus f. psammodes
intro: Phaseolus panduratus f. psammodes, also known as sand pea, is a variety of the common bean plant that has been found to possess medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phaseolus panduratus f. psammodes include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
